name: Explore Codebase
desc: High-level map of an unfamiliar codebase
prompt: /goat-debug investigate this codebase. If I don't tell you where to focus, produce a high-level map: entry points, main domains, external integrations, test coverage hotspots. Read actual files — don't guess from names. Ask me what area to drill into next.

name: Diagnose Error
desc: Diagnosis only — stops before proposing a fix
prompt: /goat-debug diagnose an error. Ask me to paste the error message, stack trace, and what I was doing when it triggered. Stop before proposing a fix — I want to understand the cause first. Ask questions if the stack trace is ambiguous or the repro steps are unclear.

name: Fix Bug
desc: Diagnosis through to fix and post-fix verification
prompt: /goat-debug fix a bug end-to-end. Ask me for expected vs actual behaviour and steps to reproduce. Don't expand scope to "while we're here" refactors — fix only what's necessary. Call out any regression risk for related code paths before declaring done.

name: Code Review
desc: Two-pass review of recent changes with MUST/SHOULD/MAY severity
prompt: /goat-review my recent changes on this branch. Flag findings as MUST (blocks merge), SHOULD (fix before it spreads), or MAY (nitpick — skip if time is tight). Ask questions if the scope is unclear.

name: Quality Audit
desc: Area audit of the most-changed files by commit frequency
prompt: /goat-review audit the most-changed files in this repo. Find hotspots by commit frequency, then review for code smells, complexity creep, test gaps, and unclear ownership. Prioritise findings by risk × change frequency — a small smell in a frequently-changed file beats a big one in a stable file.

name: Review Uncommitted
desc: Pre-commit gate — MUST findings only, no fix suggestions
prompt: /goat-review my uncommitted changes as a pre-commit gate. Flag MUST findings only — bugs, security issues, broken tests, missing null checks, regression risks. Skip SHOULD and MAY. If no MUST findings, say "ship it" and stop. Don't offer fixes — I want to decide.

name: Review Instructions
desc: Audit agent instruction files for drift and contradictions
prompt: /goat-review audit my agent instruction files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md) for drift. Check for: stale references, contradictions between files, missing verification gates, broken cross-references. Verify every claim by reading the actual file or running the command — do not assume it's correct because it's written down.

name: Critique GOAT Flow
desc: Read-only audit of the goat-flow installation
prompt: /goat-review audit the goat-flow installation in this project. Read-only scope: AGENTS.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, .goat-flow/config.yaml, .goat-flow/skill-reference/, installed skills under .claude/skills/, .agents/skills/, .github/skills/, plus hooks and active-agent config. Check for setup quality, contradictions, stale documentation, and duplicated content.

name: Plan Feature
desc: Feature brief to structured plan with scope boundaries
prompt: /goat-plan a new feature. Ask me for the feature brief (problem being solved, users, success criteria, constraints). Produce a plan with scope boundaries (in vs out), affected files/modules, spike/POC needs, assumption list, and risk callouts. Not a task list yet — that's the next step. Ask questions if the brief is ambiguous.

name: Break Into Milestones
desc: Milestone task files with testable exit criteria
prompt: /goat-plan break this into milestones. Use the feature plan from context; if none, ask which feature. Each milestone gets: goal (one sentence), exit criteria (testable), key tasks. M1 is always a spike validating the riskiest assumption. No milestone longer than 3-5 days of work — split if larger.

name: Plan Refactor
desc: Sequenced refactor plan with blast-radius analysis
prompt: /goat-plan a refactor. Ask me what to restructure and the expected blast radius. Map actual callers, tests, and dependencies — use grep, don't guess. Propose a sequenced plan that preserves behaviour at each step. Flag any API-breaking changes that require caller updates.

name: Critique a Plan
desc: SKEPTIC/ANALYST/STRATEGIST critique of a plan
prompt: /goat-critique this plan. Apply the SKEPTIC, ANALYST, and STRATEGIST lenses. Concrete findings, not vibes. Ask which plan to critique if none in context.

name: Critique an Artifact
desc: Multi-lens critique of an assessment, strategy, or review findings
prompt: /goat-critique an artifact. Ask me what to critique (assessment, strategy, review findings, test strategy, architecture proposal) if nothing is in context. Flag anything stated without evidence, any alternative not considered, and any dependency that could invalidate the whole thing.

name: Pressure-Test a Skill
desc: RED/GREEN/REFACTOR skill TDD using the quality-testing protocol
prompt: /goat-critique pressure-test a goat-flow skill using .goat-flow/skill-reference/skill-quality-testing.md. Ask me which skill. Ask questions if the scenario scope is unclear.

name: Targeted UI Testing Plan
desc: Code-change risk analysis with targeted UI testing tasks
prompt:
/goat-qa Use gh to carefully read the GitHub issue link and all comments, then analyse the code changes in the pull request. Git checkout the branch and analyse the code changes locally to determine a highly targeted testing plan.

If I haven't provided the git branch, GitHub issue URL, or PR URL, ask me for them before proceeding.

Do NOT include what the developer has already tested, or what will be covered by static code analysis testing or view-all-pages acceptance testing.

Give me a targeted UI testing plan based on code-change risk. Aim for roughly 3-10 tasks of around 5-10 minutes each, but adjust the number and size to fit the actual risk — fewer tasks for small changes, more for complex ones. Present as a markdown table with columns: Test Task, Why Important.

Ask questions if required to achieve the goal of a targeted testing plan that saves time by not testing what is already covered, but still tests based on code-change risk. UI testing only on localdev.

name: Coverage Audit
desc: Test coverage depth audit for a codebase area
prompt: /goat-qa audit test coverage in a codebase area. Ask which area — default to the most-changed files. For each file, map existing tests, assertion depth (shallow/mid/deep), uncovered branches, and risk level. Don't conflate "file has tests" with "file is well-tested".

name: Regression Guard
desc: Test capturing the invariant violated by a recent bug
prompt: /goat-qa regression guard for a recent bug fix. Ask me for the bug details (what broke, root cause, fix applied) if not in context. Write a test that captures the invariant that was violated — not just the happy path. Place it near existing tests for the affected module.

name: User Flow Diagram
desc: Mermaid flow diagram traced from actual code paths
prompt: /goat-qa produce a Mermaid user-flow diagram for QA handoff. Ask which feature if not in context. Trace actual code paths (controllers, services, external calls) — don't guess based on feature name. Include entry points, decision branches, external calls, error paths, and success states.

name: Security Assessment
desc: Full threat assessment with CONFIRMED/PROBABLE/THEORETICAL findings
prompt: /goat-security full threat assessment of this project. Read the codebase, config, and deployment surface — not just package manifests. Classify findings as CONFIRMED (reproduced or clearly exploitable), PROBABLE (strong evidence, needs validation), or THEORETICAL (attack class exists but exploitation path unclear).

name: Compliance Gap Check
desc: Compliance-mode assessment mapped to a specific framework
prompt: /goat-security compliance-mode assessment. Ask me which framework (SOC 2, HIPAA, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, etc.). Map each applicable control to evidence in the codebase — quote the actual clause, show the actual implementation or absence. Don't fabricate compliance claims — if you can't find evidence, say "not assessed".

name: Dependency Scan
desc: CVE and supply-chain check on dependencies
prompt: /goat-security scan dependencies for known CVEs and supply-chain risks. Cross-reference the lockfile for exact versions. Flag any dependencies with no updates in 2+ years or from unmaintained sources.

name: Critique Security Findings
desc: Multi-lens critique challenging overstated or missing evidence
prompt: /goat-critique this security assessment. Which findings are overstated, which are missing evidence, which rely on theoretical attack chains? What's the actual exploitability and blast radius? What structural fixes would prevent whole classes of issues? Don't rubber-stamp.
